comments.body:
https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/pull/123#discussion_r68456683

Personally I am using `make binary-local` exclusively, I really care very little.
Same here, I build locally and integration tests are being run in Fedora, so no big deal to me either
Well lets add it to the todo list to set this up to build on Fedora.  What environment variables need to be set to build locally?

> Well lets add it to the todo list to set this up to build on Fedora.

we could make something like the patch we carry for docker to build using whatever base distro you're using
Sounds good to me.  And default to a Red Hat based Distro...

For ubuntu xenial you just need to run: 
`sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y golang git-core libgpgme11-dev`
Then you can use make binary-local no container needed.
Is it possible to run skopeo from an image w/o a linux distro? If so, I'd actually prefer for the resulting image to just the exe.

It would be possible to create a container image containing a (subset of) a Linux distribution + the skopeo binary, but it wouldn't be trivially convenient to run (e.g. it uses the docker and OpenShift login status in $HOME, so those would have to be e.g. mounted in as volumes).

The current `Makefile` doesn’t do that, though.
No one has converted this to a Fedora image.  I will assign to a Intern.
